content.full_text:
Alan H. Friedenthal, Esq.
4614 Vesper Avenue
Sherman Oaks, California 91403
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-97-220
Dear Mr. Friedenthal:
This letter is a response to your request for advice on the behalf of the Georgia Mercer
regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”)
1 as amended by Proposition 208.
QUESTION
May an affiliated labor organization contribute up to the contribution limit imposed by
Section 85301 if another affiliated labor organization has contributed the limit?
CONCLUSION
All payments made by a labor organization established, financed, maintained, or
controlled by any labor organization shall be considered to be made by a single person and must
be within the contribution limits imposed by Section 85301.
FACTS
You have been retained by Georgia Mercer with respect to the above inquiry.
ANALYSIS
Proposition 208, among other things, imposed limits on the amount of contributions a
Government Code sections 81000
- 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109
-
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES
COMMISSION
May 8, 1997
428 3 STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


File No. A-97-220
Page 2
candidate may accept from any person.
2 (Section 85301.) Section 85311 provides:
“All payments made by a person established, financed, maintained,
or controlled by any business entity, labor organization,
association, political party, or any other person or group ofpersons
shall be considered to be made by a single person.”
The Commission plans to address whether a regulation is needed to interpret this section
in the near future. Until that time, the plain language of Section 85311 will control in the context
of affiliated entities. Therefore, if a labor organization is established, financed, maintained, or
controlled by any other labor organization, the cumulative amount the affiliated organizations
may give must be under the limits imposed by Section 85301.
Ifyou have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Marte Castafios
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosure
SGC:MC:ak
2 “Person means an individual, proprietorship, firm, partnership, joint venture, syndicate, business trust,
company, corporation, limited liability company, association, committee, and any other organization or group of
persons acting in concert.” (Section 82047.).
For you information, I have enclosed a copy of the Shaw Advice Letter, 1-97-125, where a local chapter
of an umbrella organization was determined to be affiliated within the defmition of Section 85311.

Section 85301 if another affiliated labor organization has contributed the limit?

CONCLUSION: All payments made by a labor organization established, financed, maintained, or
